content: Tax-loss harvesting sells losing positions to realize capital losses, offsetting capital gains or up to $3,000 of ordinary income annually. Unused losses carry forward indefinitely. The wash-sale rule prohibits repurchasing substantially identical securities within 30 days—violating disallows the loss. Workaround: sell S&P 500 ETF, buy total market ETF (similar exposure, not 'substantially identical'). Robo-advisors automate tax-loss harvesting, adding 0.5-1% annual value for high-turnover portfolios. Benefits scale with account size and tax bracket. Considerations: transaction costs, bid-ask spreads, and potential long-term tax consequences if harvested assets later appreciate significantly. Best suited for taxable accounts; irrelevant in tax-advantaged retirement accounts.
